actors.text: Sip W cups (word-typed) and N cups (numerical). Need 3 of each before voting.

stages.questionText: What KIND of thing does each cup carry?

doorways.text: the names of the individuals themselves
doorways.reflection: {N} of {TOTAL} of you ({PCT}%) reached for the names -- but a cup's VALUE is the measurement, not the unit it was measured on.
{N} of {TOTAL} of you ({PCT}%) thought conditions -- those come later, in Unit 3 experimental design. Here the cups carry observed values.

doorways.text: numerical variables that happen to be words
doorways.reflection: {N} of {TOTAL} of you ({PCT}%) blurred the line -- but numerical means you can do arithmetic on it. Word labels like 'red' or 'tall' cannot be averaged.
{N} of {TOTAL} of you ({PCT}%) picked names -- names are LABELS for the units, not the measured variables themselves.
{N} of {TOTAL} of you ({PCT}%) chose rankings -- rankings are ordinal but still qualitative. Quantitative values support arithmetic; rankings do not.
{N} of {TOTAL} of you ({PCT}%) said categorical -- numbers CAN be categorical if used as codes (jersey #), but here the N cups carry measurements.

doorways.text: the unit on which the variables are measured

doorways.reflection: {N} of {TOTAL} of you ({PCT}%) picked a measurement -- that's a VALUE of a variable, not the individual it was measured on.
{N} of {TOTAL} of you ({PCT}%) chose the dataset -- but the dataset is the COLLECTION of all individuals. One individual is one unit in that collection.

actors.text: [Reflection text placeholder -- filled at runtime from the chosen door's `reflection` field]

completion.rule: any correct QuestionDoor switch pressed by >= 1/3 of online players, then any Player walks onto Goal
